nodes.description:
Read a file from disk and inject its contents into the message.

Props:
  - ``filename``     : path of the file to read. Can be a literal string, a flow
                       variable, or overridden by ``msg['filename']`` / ``msg['path']``.
  - ``target_field`` : message field to write the loaded data into (default: ``"data"``).
  - ``mode``         : deserialization format:
                       ``"auto"`` (default) — infer from extension:
                         ``.json`` → JSON, ``.bin``/``.dat``/``.npy``/``.raw`` → binary,
                         anything else → text.
                       ``"json"``   — parse as JSON, result is a dict/list/scalar.
                       ``"text"``   — read as plain text string.
                       ``"binary"`` — read raw bytes; ``.npy`` files are loaded with
                                      ``numpy.load``.
  - ``encoding``     : text/JSON file encoding (default: ``"utf-8"``).

Output:
  Forwards the message with ``msg[target_field]`` set to the loaded data and
  ``msg['filename']`` set to the path that was read.
nodes.label: ListDir

nodes.description:
Iterate over an array or dict and emit each element as a separate message.

Props:
  - `input_array`: field name in msg containing the array or dict (default: 'items')
  - `item_field`: field name to store current item/value in output msg (default: 'item').
                  If empty string ("") and preserve_payload=True, reveals object fields 
                  directly into msg (only works if item is a dict)
  - `key_field`: field name to store the dict key in output msg (default: 'key').
                 Only used when iterating over a dict; ignored for lists/tuples.
  - `index_field`: field name to store the 0-based numeric position (default: 'index')
  - `preserve_payload`: if True, keeps original msg and adds item/index fields; 
                        if False, replaces entire msg with item (default: True)
  - `wait_on_buffer_full`: if True, wait for buffer space instead of raising error (default: True)

Emits one message per element on the 'out' slot sequentially.

Dict iteration:
  - Iterates over dict.items(); each message gets key_field=key and item_field=value.
  - index_field holds the numeric 0-based position (not the key).
  - If item_field is empty string and the value is a dict, its fields are merged into msg.

List/tuple iteration:
  - Same as before; key_field is not set.
  - item_field empty string: reveals dict item fields directly into msg.

Note: Schema validation is skipped since field names are dynamic (configurable via props).
The node expects an array or dict at msg[input_array] and produces msg[item_field] and msg[index_field].
nodes.label: ForEach

nodes.description:
Read a file from disk and inject its contents into the message.

Props:
  - ``filename``     : path of the file to read. Can be a literal string, a flow
                       variable, or overridden by ``msg['filename']`` / ``msg['path']``.
  - ``target_field`` : message field to write the loaded data into (default: ``"data"``).
  - ``mode``         : deserialization format:
                       ``"auto"`` (default) — infer from extension:
                         ``.json`` → JSON, ``.bin``/``.dat``/``.npy``/``.raw`` → binary,
                         anything else → text.
                       ``"json"``   — parse as JSON, result is a dict/list/scalar.
                       ``"text"``   — read as plain text string.
                       ``"binary"`` — read raw bytes; ``.npy`` files are loaded with
                                      ``numpy.load``.
  - ``encoding``     : text/JSON file encoding (default: ``"utf-8"``).

Output:
  Forwards the message with ``msg[target_field]`` set to the loaded data and
  ``msg['filename']`` set to the path that was read.
nodes.label: ImportData

nodes.description:
Counterpart to ForEach: collect all per-item messages back into one message.

ForEach stamps each emitted message with three hidden fields:
  - ``_foreach_id``    – unique UUID for this array-processing run
  - ``_foreach_index`` – 0-based position of the item inside the original array
  - ``_foreach_total`` – total number of items emitted for this run

ForEachCollect buffers messages keyed by ``_foreach_id``.  When all
``_foreach_total`` items have arrived it reassembles them and emits a
single message whose array field contains every item (optionally sorted
back to the original order).

Props:
  - ``item_field`` (str): field in each buffered message that holds the
    processed item.  If ``""`` the entire message dict (minus the hidden
    ``_foreach_*`` keys) is collected as the item.  Default: ``"item"``.
  - ``index_field`` (str): override the index key to use for ordering;
    normally you should leave this as ``"_foreach_index"``.  Default:
    ``"_foreach_index"``.
  - ``output_field`` (str): message field to write the collected array
    into.  Default: ``"items"``.
  - ``collects`` (dict): multi-field collection mapping.  Each key is an
    output field name; its value is a list of source item-field names to
    read from each buffered message.

    - One source field  → output is a flat list of values: ``[v, v, ...]``
    - Multiple fields   → output is a list of dicts: ``[{f1: v, f2: v}, ...]``

    When ``collects`` is set it takes precedence over ``item_field`` /
    ``output_field``.

    Example::

        {
          "collects": {
            "positions":    ["position"],
            "measurements": ["length", "max_width", "area"]
          }
        }

  - ``preserve_first`` (bool): if True, the metadata of the *first*
    (index-0) upstream message is used as the base for the output
    message (minus the per-item payload).  If False only the
    ``output_field`` is carried.  Default: True.
  - ``sort`` (bool): sort collected items by their ``_foreach_index``
    before writing.  Default: True.

Output slots:
  - ``out``   – emitted when all items have been collected.
  - ``empty`` – emitted instead when the original array was empty
    (``_foreach_total == 0``).

Example – double every length in blob_measurements::

    ForEach (input_array=blob_measurements, item_field=blob)
      → ModifyMessage (blob.length *= 2)
    ForEachCollect (item_field=blob, output_field=blob_measurements)
nodes.label: ForEachCollect

nodes.description:
Render a Mustache-like template into a string and write it to a msg field.

Props:
    - `template`: template string (uses `{{key}}` or `{{nested.key}}` to interpolate from msg)
    - `output`: dot-path in msg where to write the rendered string (default: 'result')
    - `default`: value to substitute when a key is missing (default: '')
    - simple ternary: `{{flag ? "OK" : "NOK"}}` (flag read from msg/params)

    Example:
            props.template = "Tile {{x}},{{y}} area={{area}}"
            msg = {'x': 10, 'y': 20, 'area': 123}
            -> msg['result'] = "Tile 10,20 area=123"
    
    Warnings:
            - Logs warning when interpolation keys are missing from message
            - Increments node warning_count for monitoring in dashboard

    Filters / Pipes:
            - You can apply simple filters using a pipe (`|`) inside the moustache
                expression, similar to Angular pipes. Filters are applied left-to-right.
            - Example: `{{ timestamp | date:'YYYY/MM/DD' }}` will format a unix
                timestamp or ISO datetime into `2026/02/01` (when timestamp corresponds).
            - The `date` filter accepts a format string using tokens: `YYYY, YY, MM,
                DD, hh/HH, mm, ss` which are mapped to `strftime` equivalents.
            - Supported inputs: numeric unix timestamps (seconds or milliseconds),
                ISO formatted datetime strings, or Python `datetime` objects.
nodes.label: StringTemplate

subflow_1773072242623.name: subflow_1773072242623

subflow_1773072242623.description: Akkumuliert Masken und verwendet dann einen statitischen Wert für die Maske

nodes.description:
Accumulate binary images across messages by counting 255-pixels per position.

Each arriving image contributes +1 to pixels that are 255 (or > 0).
The internal accumulator is a uint16 array so up to 65535 images can be stacked.
The output image is the raw count (uint16) stored in ``output_field``.

Use a ``Threshold`` node downstream to binarize the count image.

Props:
  - ``source``      : message field containing the binary input image (default: ``"payload"``).
  - ``output_field``: message field to write the accumulated count image to (default: ``"payload"``).
  - ``count_field`` : message field to write the number of accumulated frames to (default: ``"acc_count"``).
  - ``reset_field`` : optional message field name; if the field is truthy the accumulator is reset
                      *before* adding the current frame (default: ``None`` – never resets).
  - ``emit_every``  : emit a message every N accumulated images (default: 1).
                      Use e.g. ``10`` to only emit after every 10th image.

Outputs ``out`` with the count image and ``acc_count`` filled in.
nodes.label: ImageAccumulate

nodes.description:
Evaluate a mathematical formula and write the result into a message field.

Variables in the formula are resolved from the message and from flow/global
variables using the same scoping conventions as StringTemplate:

  - ``field``         – reads ``msg['field']`` (plain identifier)
  - ``msg.field``     – explicit msg field (supports dot-paths: ``msg.data.value``)
  - ``flow.name``     – flow-scoped variable
  - ``vars.name``     – alias for ``flow.name``
  - ``subflow.name``  – alias for ``flow.name``
  - ``global.name``   – global-scoped variable

Math functions available in formulas
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
``abs``, ``round``, ``min``, ``max``, ``sum``, ``pow``,
``sqrt``, ``cbrt``, ``exp``, ``log``, ``log2``, ``log10``,
``floor``, ``ceil``, ``trunc``,
``sin``, ``cos``, ``tan``, ``asin``, ``acos``, ``atan``, ``atan2``,
``degrees``, ``radians``, ``hypot``,
``isnan``, ``isinf``, ``isfinite``

Constants: ``pi``, ``e``, ``inf``, ``nan``

Props
~~~~~
- ``formula``   : expression string, e.g. ``"(temperature - 32) * 5 / 9"`` or
                  ``"sqrt(a**2 + b**2)"`` or ``"flow.offset + msg.value * 2"``
- ``output``    : dot-path in msg to write the result (default: ``"result"``)
- ``default``   : value written when the formula raises an error (default: ``null``)
- ``variables`` : optional dict of named constants / variable bindings that
                  override anything from msg, e.g.
                  ``{"factor": 1.8, "offset": {"scope": "flow", "var": "my_offset"}}``
                  Variable-ref dicts with ``scope`` + ``var`` keys are resolved
                  automatically.

Examples
~~~~~~~~
Convert Fahrenheit → Celsius::

    formula:  "(temperature - 32) * 5 / 9"
    output:   "celsius"

Pythagorean hypotenuse::

    formula:  "sqrt(dx**2 + dy**2)"
    output:   "distance"

Mix msg field and flow variable::

    formula:  "flow.base_price * quantity * (1 - discount / 100)"
    output:   "total"
nodes.label: MathFormula

nodes.description:
Pixel-wise arithmetic and bitwise operations between two images.

Props:
  - ``operation``    : one of ``add``, ``subtract``, ``absdiff``, ``and``, ``or``,
                       ``xor``, ``not``, ``blend``.
  - ``source_a``     : message field for the first image (default: ``"payload"``).
  - ``source_b``     : message field for the second image (default: ``"mask"``).
                       Ignored for the ``not`` operation.
                       If the two images have different sizes, ``source_b`` is resized
                       to match ``source_a``.
                       If the two images have different channel counts, ``source_b`` is
                       converted to match ``source_a``.
  - ``scalar``       : integer 0-255.  When set, ``add`` / ``subtract`` use this constant
                       value instead of ``source_b``.
  - ``alpha``        : weight for ``source_a`` in ``blend`` mode (default: ``0.5``).
  - ``beta``         : weight for ``source_b`` in ``blend`` mode (default: ``0.5``).
  - ``normalize``    : if ``True``, normalize the result image to the full 0-255 range.
  - ``output_field`` : message field to write the result to (default: ``"payload"``).

Operations:
  - ``add``      → ``cv2.add(a, b)`` (saturating)
  - ``subtract`` → ``cv2.subtract(a, b)`` (saturating)
  - ``absdiff``  → ``cv2.absdiff(a, b)``
  - ``and``      → ``cv2.bitwise_and(a, b)``
  - ``or``       → ``cv2.bitwise_or(a, b)``
  - ``xor``      → ``cv2.bitwise_xor(a, b)``
  - ``not``      → ``cv2.bitwise_not(a)``  (single-operand)
  - ``blend``    → ``cv2.addWeighted(a, alpha, b, beta, 0)``
nodes.label: ImageArithmetic

nodes.description:
Threshold an image (or count image from ImageAccumulate) to produce a binary result.

Supports uint8 and uint16 inputs (e.g. count images from ``ImageAccumulate``).

Props:
  - ``threshold``   : threshold value (default: 128; for uint16 count images use the count, e.g. 3).
  - ``polarity``    : ``'higher'`` → pixels *above* threshold become 255 (default);
                      ``'lower'``  → pixels *below* threshold become 255.
  - ``source``      : input message field (default: ``"payload"``).
  - ``output_field``: output message field (default: ``"payload"``).
  - ``type``        : OpenCV threshold type string (default: ``"binary"``):
                      ``binary``, ``binary_inv``, ``trunc``, ``tozero``, ``tozero_inv``.
                      When ``polarity`` is ``'lower'`` and type is ``'binary'``, ``binary_inv``
                      is used automatically.

Output image is always uint8 (0 / 255 for binary types).
nodes.label: Threshold
